item.title: Basal plasmacytosis and eosinophilia for distinguishing inflammatory bowel disease from gastrointestinal tuberculosis on mucosal biopsy.
item.description: Journal of clinical pathology - Accurate distinction of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and gastrointestinal tuberculosis (GITB) on mucosal biopsies remains challenging, especially in South-East Asia. Our previous meta-analysis ...

item.title: Respiratory syncytial virus incidence and clinical outcomes in patients with inflammatory bowel disease: a National Veterans Affairs cohort study.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Data describing the incidence and clinical outcomes of respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) infection among patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) are limited. We conducted a nationwide retros...

item.title: Dietary B vitamin intake and risk of ulcerative colitis: a case-control study.
item.description: European journal of gastroenterology & hepatology - A meta-analysis showed that serum folate concentrations, but not vitamin B12 concentrations, are lower in patients with ulcerative colitis compared to healthy controls. However, no epidemiological ...

item.title: Auditory Involvement in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: Clinical Predictors and Prognostic Implications.
item.description: Otology & neurotology : official publication of the American Otological Society, American Neurotology Society [and] European Academy of Otology and Neurotology - To investigate the prevalence, characteristics, and clinical significance of hearing loss as an extraintestinal manifestation (EIM) in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Cross-sectiona...

item.title: Clinical and immunological factors associated with gastrointestinal involvement in common variable immunodeficiency: a real-world study.
item.description: BMC immunology - Gastrointestinal (GI) involvement is a common and clinically significant manifestation of common variable immunodeficiency (CVID); however, its relationship with immunological parameters remains in...

item.title: Impact of body mass index on length of stay in patients with inflammatory bowel disease: A retrospective study.
item.description: Nutrition in clinical practice : official publication of the American Society for Parenteral and Enteral Nutrition - The association between body mass index (BMI) and clinical outcomes in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) remains controversial. We aimed to investigate whether BMI independently predicts length of s...

item.title: Letter: Does the Association Between Mild Endoscopic Activity and Adverse Outcomes Justify Treatment Escalation in Older Adults With Inflammatory Bowel Disease? Authors' Reply.
Association Between Keratoconus and Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
item.description: Cornea - To investigate an association between inflammatory bowel disease and keratoconus. Keratoconus can be difficult to diagnose early, and because of its progressive nature, early treatment is beneficia...

item.title: Dysfunction of mitochondria in intestinal epithelial cells: a key player in the pathogenesis of inflammatory bowel diseases.
item.description: Gastroenterology report - Review - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD) are a group of chronic gastrointestinal disorders with increasing incidence and prevalence that represent a major challenge for healthcare systems worldwide. Owing...

item.title: Prevalence and Clinical Correlates of Avoidant/Restrictive Food Intake Disorder (ARFID) Among Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Systematic Review.
item.description: Journal of clinical psychology in medical settings - Review - Avoidant/restrictive food intake disorder (ARFID) is an eating disorder characterized by inadequate or restricted food intake unrelated to body image or weight concerns. ARFID is frequently observe...

item.title: GLP-1 receptor agonists and clinical outcomes in adults with Crohn's disease and obesity: a propensity score-matched real-world cohort study.
item.description: Scientific reports - Obesity affects 15%-40% of adults with Crohn's disease (CD) and is associated with worse outcomes. GLP-1 receptor agonists (GLP-1RAs) have pleiotropic anti-inflammatory effects, yet their clinical ...

item.title: Teclistamab for treatment-refractory autoimmune diseases: a multicentre case series.
item.description: Annals of the rheumatic diseases - This study aimed to evaluate the safety and efficacy of teclistamab, a T cell-redirecting bispecific antibody targeting B-cell maturation antigen, in a case series of severe autoimmune diseases. Da...

item.title: Letter: Does the Association Between Mild Endoscopic Activity and Adverse Outcomes Justify Treatment Escalation in Older Adults With Inflammatory Bowel Disease?
Recent advances in the discovery of small-molecule inhibitors for inflammatory bowel disease therapy.
item.description: European journal of medicinal chemistry - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic condition characterized by recurrent intestinal ulceration. The rising global incidence of IBD in recent years has increased disease burden and highlig...
